Petition For Instructions Regarding Payment Of Additional Premiums For Life Insurance Funded To Settlor-S Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Aishlin O-Coyle (Trustee).
Deborah Fox for Gwendolyn Maguire (Trustor).

Mr. Schultz stated that the issue is that they still don't have full access to the policy and will not until the premium is paid in full, that the first notice they received indicated that they needed to pay $28,000.00 to keep the policy in force for one month, or $60,000.00 for one year, that a week later they received another notice stating that they had to pay $16,000.00 by August 17th, or they recommended a payment of $17,000.00. Mr. Schultz also stated that the first option covers everything, that he is surprised Ms. Harrington isn't here, provided a summary of last weeks hearing and stated that they are working on an agreement regarding Steven residing at the condo. The Court stated that it would be more comfortable going forward if it had a statement from Steven. Ms. Fox stated that she discussed the pros and cons with Ms. Maguire, that Ms. Maguire understands there is no good answer, that Ms. Maguire is comfortable with the Trustee and the Court making this decision, and that if the Trustee is allowed to preserve the policy, then there may be some options to sell the policy and at least recoup some of the money. Mr. Schultz stated that they prefers doing the $60,000.00 payment for timing purposes and explore life settlement options, and that it would be Steven's decision. The Court raised the issue of paying the $60,000.00 and getting information without paying the November 2020 additional $40,000.00. Ms. O'Coyle stated that there may be an option to pay monthly, but she needs to get permission from John Hancock. Mr. Schultz stated that the $60,000.00 covers to November 17th, that $40,000.00 is the annual premium going forward, that they first got a notice with a date of June 5th, that the notice goes to Steven and then to them, and that there is a significant $5,600.00 monthly expense. Ms. O'Coyle stated that this policy has been flagged as a redline policy, that she would rather get approval for all of it and then try to get approval for a monthly payment, and that if they pay the full amount, they do not get a refund. The Court granted the Petition for Instructions Regarding Payment of Additional Premiums for Life Insurance, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Aishlin O'Coyle (Trustee).

Deborah Fox for and with Gwen Maguire (Trustor).
Lori Maguire also present.

The Court noted that there is concern with regard to the remaining assets in the trust and concerns about what has happened in the past. Ms. Fox stated that Ms. Maguire reviewed the petition for instructions, that Ms. Maguire is very clear that she would like her Son and Daughter-in-Law to move in, that they discussed the issue of them paying rent below market value and it affecting her income, and that Ms. Maguire still wants her Son to move into the condo. Mr. Schultz stated that the Settlor will be 90, that the liquid assets will run out in four years, that if the condo is sold before her death there will be a large capital gains, that a new issue has arisen regarding the life insurance, that it requires a $40,000.00 premium and that they now have to pay $60,000.00 or she will lose it. Ms. Fox stated that she has discussed this with Ms. Maguire, but it is a very complex issue. Mr. Schultz stated that he has concerns, that they have a proposal that the Son will pay for all of the costs, that the concern is what will happen if the condo needs to be sold and that he would like to have a written agreement. Ms. Harrington stated that if they anticipate that the outcome is that the property will be sold, the value is decreased if they have a tenant, that if Steve is an invitee and is paying for some of the costs it saves the Trustor money, and that she thinks her Client will enter into an agreement that he is an invitee and not a tenant, and has no tenant rights. Ms. Harrington also stated that she does not see why this would need Court approval, that her Client's biggest concern is his Mother's quality of life and spending time with her, that it is concerning that she will run out of money given the size of her estate, and that she supposes they can draft an agreement of Steven being an invitee and amend the petition. Mr. Schultz stated that the Trustor is in a facility and receiving good care, that the reason she was placed in a facility was because she had over 10 falls while at home and that in order to remain in this facility she requires a certain level of care. The Petition for Instructions Regarding Rental of Trust's Condominium is continued to September 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to work out an agreement and for filing of an amended petition. The Court reminds all parties that the focus needs to be on what is in the best interest for Ms. Maguire and what needs to be done to ensure that her care needs are provided for.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
